stemHtml: According to the European Regulations on Air Operations, an Operator shall NOT operate a turbine propeller powered, or an aeroplane with a maximum certificated Take-off mass in excess of 5700 kg, or having a maximum Operational Passenger Seating Configuration (MOPSC) of more than nine seats, or a turbojet powered aeroplane, unless it is equipped with an altitude alerting system capable of alerting the flight crew when…

options.a: Flying over terrain that exceeds the maximum one engine inoperative cruising altitude.
options.b: The cabin altitude exceeds 10 000ft (cabin pressure less than 700 hPa).
options.c: Exceeding the service ceiling of the aeroplane.
